author: Nathan Gotch
topic_filter: AI SEO / GEO / LLM SEO / AI content strategy / AI search

posts.text:
Most of the advice about "AI SEO" is overcomplicated nonsense.

At the end of the day, the best way to get your brand visible in AI answers is still to do good SEO and focus on search everywhere optimization.

That's the game.

If your brand has strong visibility across search surfaces and core retrieval sources, you're far more likely to show up in AI answers.

SEO is the lead domino.

But I don't just mean SEO on your website, even though that's the right place to start.

➡ You should also be thinking about SEO on YouTube.

➡ You should be thinking about visibility across every important retrieval source.

And the number one retrieval source is still search.

Your brand needs to be present on the sources these systems are actually pulling from.

I use Rankability to quickly find retrieval source gaps for any topic.

These become your outreach targets.

That gives you a clear path: increase your visibility on the retrieval sources that are already influencing AI answers.

Do that consistently, and you give yourself a much better chance of showing up more often.

There's no real secret here.

➡ It's consistent technical SEO on your website.

➡ It's building topic authority on your website.

➡ It's extending that authority across your other owned assets.

➡ And it's earning visibility off-site on the sources that AI systems actually trust and retrieve from.

That's how you win.

Building a solo AI-driven SEO agency sounds great...

Until you fire your team, absorb all their workload, and turn your business into a 12-hour-a-day job just to squeeze out more profit.

Not exactly glamorous.

AI and agents should empower your team to deliver better work. They shouldn't turn your agency into a founder bottleneck that can't scale.

Quick reminder for anyone doing AI SEO:

"Prompt volume" is fake.

Claude Opus and GPT 5.4 can absolutely build a high-level SEO plan for any business.

But planning isn't what determines success.

That's like making a plan to become great at baseball and never stepping on the field. 

You can create the perfect strategy, but SEO has always come down to execution.

❌ It's not about fancy plans.
❌ It's not about 100-page audits.

That's never been the difference between SEOs who succeed and those who don't.

The real difference has always been the quality of execution.

Perplexity will now do your SEO! 

Good luck 😈
